notes: Time.fixedDeltaTime defaults to 0.02s (50Hz). Time.deltaTime for frame-rate-independent movement. Use Time.timeScale for pause.
UE5 uses variable tick by default. Enable substepping for deterministic physics. TickInterval can throttle per-actor tick rate.

code:
# Godot's game loop:
# _physics_process(delta) â€” fixed timestep (default 60Hz)
# _process(delta) â€” once per frame (variable)
func _physics_process(delta: float) -> void:
    # Physics and gameplay logic
    pass
func _process(delta: float) -> void:
    # Rendering, UI, input
    pass
notes: Physics tick rate set in Project Settings > Physics > Common > Physics Ticks Per Second. Use delta for frame-independent math.
Built-in since Unity 2021. Pre-warm with Get/Release loops at scene start. maxSize silently destroys overflow objects.
Disable tick and collision on pooled actors. Use SetActorTickEnabled(false). Consider UE5's FObjectPool for UObject pooling.
Use set_process(false) and set_physics_process(false) on released objects. Pre-instantiate during _ready() for stutter-free gameplay.
Always unsubscribe in OnDestroy to prevent memory leaks. For designer-facing events, use UnityEvent with Inspector binding.
Use DYNAMIC delegates for Blueprint binding, non-dynamic for C++-only (faster). Always unbind in EndPlay.

code:
# Godot Signals (first-class observer pattern):
signal score_changed(new_score: int)

# Emit: score_changed.emit(new_score)
# Connect: emitter.score_changed.connect(_on_score_changed)
# Disconnect: emitter.score_changed.disconnect(_on_score_changed)
notes: Signals are Godot's native observer pattern. Connect in _ready(), disconnect automatically on free(). Use @export for editor-connectable signals.
Store commands in a Stack for undo. Use for input replay, networked action queues, and level editor operations.
UE5 Editor has built-in transaction system (GEditor->BeginTransaction). For gameplay undo, roll your own command stack.

code:
class_name Command extends RefCounted
func execute() -> void: pass
func undo() -> void: pass

class_name MoveCommand extends Command
var target: Node3D
var old_pos: Vector3
var new_pos: Vector3
func execute() -> void: target.position = new_pos
func undo() -> void: target.position = old_pos
notes: Use an Array[Command] as a history stack. Godot's UndoRedo class provides built-in undo for editor plugins.
For complex FSMs, use Unity's Animator as a visual state machine. Scriptable-object-based FSMs scale better than switch statements.
UE5 AI uses Behavior Trees (preferred) or State Trees (UE5.4+). Use Blueprint enums for designer-visible states.
For complex FSMs, use a State node pattern: each state is a child node with enter/exit/process methods. Godot 4 has no built-in FSM.
DOTS ECS requires Unity 2022.3+ with Entities package. Burst compiler required for performance. Hybrid renderer bridges ECS and GameObjects.
Mass Entity is UE5's ECS framework. Still experimental. Most UE5 projects use traditional Actor/Component model instead.
For serious ECS in Godot, consider GDExtension with a Rust ECS (bevy_ecs, hecs). Pure GDScript ECS is limited by interpreter speed.
Unity has no built-in BT. Popular assets: Behavior Designer, NodeCanvas. Keep trees shallow â€” deep trees are hard to debug.
UE5 BTs are the standard AI framework. Use Blackboard for shared state. EQS (Environment Query System) for spatial queries. Editor has visual BT debugger.
No built-in BT in Godot. LimboAI is the most popular addon. Alternatively, use the node tree itself as a BT structure.
Unity's physics engine uses a BVH internally. For non-physics queries (AI awareness, AoE), a simple grid is often sufficient and faster than OverlapSphere.
UE5 World Partition handles level streaming automatically. For gameplay queries, use OverlapMulti or SweepMulti. Collision channels control query scope.

code:
# Godot physics spatial queries:
var space = get_world_3d().direct_space_state
var query = PhysicsShapeQueryParameters3D.new()
query.shape = SphereShape3D.new()
query.shape.radius = 10.0
query.transform.origin = global_position
var results = space.intersect_shape(query, 32)
notes: Godot uses Jolt or GodotPhysics. For custom spatial partitioning, implement a grid in GDScript or use Area3D with monitoring enabled.
Unity's Transform already uses dirty flags internally. Apply this pattern for expensive derived data: pathfinding caches, visibility sets, LOD computations.
UE5's render thread uses dirty flags for transform updates. MarkRenderStateDirty() is the engine's version of this pattern.
Godot's notification system (NOTIFICATION_TRANSFORM_CHANGED) is the engine's dirty flag mechanism. Use for caching pathfinding, visibility, or AI queries.
Rollback requires deterministic simulation. Use fixed-point math or ensure floating-point consistency. Unity Netcode for GameObjects has prediction support.
UE5's CharacterMovement has built-in client-side prediction and server correction. Full rollback (fighting games) needs custom implementation or GGPO plugin.
Godot has no built-in rollback. SG Physics (addon) provides deterministic physics for rollback. Use SceneTree.set_multiplayer for basic networking.
Mirror's NetworkTransform v2 uses snapshot interpolation by default. Fish-Net provides TransformSmoother for similar functionality.
UE5's FRepMovement handles most interpolation automatically. Custom actors need OnRep callbacks with manual lerp.

code:
# Godot 4: MultiplayerSynchronizer with interpolation
# On server: update_interval = 0.05 (20 Hz)
# On client: interpolate between two buffered states
var state_buffer = []
var interpolation_delay = 0.1 # seconds

func _process(delta):
    if multiplayer.is_server(): return
    var render_time = Time.get_ticks_msec() / 1000.0 - interpolation_delay
    interpolate_to(render_time)
notes: Use MultiplayerSynchronizer's built-in interpolation (Godot 4.3+) when possible. Manual buffer needed for sub-100ms latency targets.
Use Fish-Net's PredictionManager for production-quality rollback. Mirror prediction requires manual input history and reconciliation.
UE5 CharacterMovement handles prediction/correction automatically. Custom actors need FSavedMove subclass for replay.
Godot has no built-in prediction. Implement manually or use GodotSteam/GDNet addon. MultiplayerSynchronizer handles authority transfer.
Mirror provides NetworkTime.rtt for RTT estimation. Valve-style lag compensation buffers 1 second of position history (tick rate * 1s frames).
UE5 provides ULagCompensationComponent for automatic rewind. Custom implementations use FRepMovement history buffer.
No built-in lag compensation in Godot. Must implement position history buffer and rewind manually. Keep 1 second of history at server tick rate.
Mirror and Fish-Net are server-authoritative by default. [Command] runs on server, [ClientRpc] broadcasts to clients. [SyncVar] auto-replicates.
UE5's networking is server-authoritative by design. Use UFUNCTION(Server) for client-to-server RPCs. DOREPLIFETIME for state replication.
Godot 4 MultiplayerAPI with set_multiplayer_authority(). @rpc annotations control who can call and reliability. Server validates all state changes.
Unity's floating point is NOT deterministic across platforms. Use fixed-point math libraries (e.g. Fix64) or BEPUphysics for deterministic simulation.
UE5 is NOT deterministic by default (PhysX, floating point). Lockstep requires replacing physics with a deterministic solver. Consider Chaos physics deterministic mode.
Godot's physics (Godot Physics and Jolt) are not guaranteed deterministic. For lockstep, use fixed-point math and avoid engine physics.
Mirror SyncVar updates trigger correction. Dead reckoning is most useful for distant entities where snapshot interpolation adds too much latency.
UE5's FRepMovement includes velocity for automatic dead reckoning. SimulatedProxy mode handles this for Characters automatically.
Godot MultiplayerSynchronizer can handle basic position replication. Dead reckoning is manual â€” add velocity to synced properties for prediction.
Mirror NetworkTransform v2 uses double-buffer interpolation. Set sendRate and interpolation settings in the component inspector.
UE5 handles entity interpolation automatically for SimulatedProxy role. Tune NetUpdateFrequency (default 100Hz) and interpolation speed.
MultiplayerSynchronizer handles basic interpolation. Manual buffer gives finer control over interpolation delay and jitter handling.
Mirror provides SpatialHashInterestManagement and DistanceInterestManagement out of the box. Override for custom AOI logic.
UE5's NetCullDistanceSquared and IsNetRelevantFor() handle interest management. Use NetPriority for bandwidth allocation within relevant set.
Godot has no built-in interest management. Implement distance checks manually and use rpc_id() to send only to relevant peers.
Mirror handles basic delta compression internally for SyncVars (only sends changed fields). Custom delta compression needed for high-frequency data.
UE5's property replication system handles delta compression automatically. DOREPLIFETIME_CONDITION adds conditional replication to reduce bandwidth further.
Godot MultiplayerSynchronizer sends full property values. Manual delta encoding needed for bandwidth optimization.
Score functions return 0-1 normalized values. Use AnimationCurve for response curves. Consider inertia to prevent rapid switching.
UE5 EQS is a built-in utility system. Combine with BT via EQS Query node. Test types: Distance, Trace, Dot, Pathfinding.
Use Callable for score/execute functions. Curve resource for response curves. Consider action cooldowns.
Reference GOAP implementation from F.E.A.R. GDC talk. Cache plans when world state unchanged. Use A* over action graph.
UE5 has no built-in GOAP. Implement custom planner or use plugins. Execute plans through BT dynamic subtrees.
Dictionary-based world state. Sort open list by cost for optimal plans. Cache plans when world state hash unchanged.
Type-safe wrappers via generics. Consider event notifications on value change. Clear stale entries on state transitions.
UE5 BlackboardComponent is tightly integrated with BT. Use BB decorators to react to value changes. Define keys in BlackboardData asset.
Signal-based notifications for reactive BT decorators. RefCounted for shared ownership. Dictionary backend.
Combine behaviors with weighted sum or priority. Use Reynolds flocking (separation + alignment + cohesion) for groups.
UE5 navigation handles most steering via NavMesh. Use custom steering for local obstacle avoidance and flocking.
Combine with NavigationAgent3D for pathfollowing + local steering. Use CharacterBody3D.velocity for applying forces.
Use NavMesh for 3D navigation. For grid/tile games, implement A* manually or use AStar2D/AStar3D. NavMeshAgent handles path smoothing.
UE5 NavigationSystem uses navmesh internally. Use EQS for smarter path selection. RecastNavMesh for runtime updates.
Godot provides AStar2D/AStar3D for graph search and NavigationAgent3D/NavigationMesh for navmesh pathfinding.
Bake navmesh in editor or at runtime with NavMeshSurface. NavMeshObstacle for dynamic obstacles. NavMeshLink for jumps/drops.
UE5 uses Recast/Detour internally. Nav mesh updates dynamically for movable geometry. Use Nav Modifiers for area costs.

code:
# Godot NavigationMesh setup
# 1. Add NavigationRegion3D node
# 2. Create NavigationMesh resource and bake
var agent = $NavigationAgent3D
agent.target_position = target_pos
var next_pos = agent.get_next_path_position()
velocity = (next_pos - global_position).normalized() * speed
move_and_slide()
notes: Godot 4 navigation is server-based. NavigationRegion3D for baked meshes. NavigationAgent3D for per-agent pathfinding with avoidance.
Track player health, ammo, time since last death, combat encounters. Use rolling averages to smooth input. L4D GDC talk is the reference.
Implement as WorldSubsystem for global access. Use data tables for intensity-to-spawn mappings. Expose tuning in editor.
Autoload singleton for global access. Signal-based spawn adjustments. Export intensity curve for designer tuning.
Attach to player CharacterController. Adjust coyoteTime (0.08-0.15s) and jumpBuffer (0.08-0.12s) for feel.
Extends CharacterBody2D. Godot uses negative Y for up. Tune coyote_time for snappy vs forgiving feel.
ActorComponent attached to Character BP. Bind bJumpPressed to Enhanced Input action. Adjust LaunchVelocity for jump height.
ICombatant interface: Speed, IsDead, OnTurnStart(). For ATB systems, use a priority queue with cooldown timers instead.

code:
# Turn queue state machine
class_name TurnManager extends Node
var turn_queue: Array[Combatant] = []
var current: Combatant = null
func start_battle(combatants: Array[Combatant]):
    turn_queue = combatants.duplicate()
    turn_queue.sort_custom(func(a,b): return a.speed > b.speed)
    next_turn()
func next_turn():
    if turn_queue.is_empty(): end_battle(); return
    current = turn_queue.pop_front()
    current.on_turn_start(self)
func end_current_turn():
    if not current.is_dead:
        turn_queue.push_back(current)
    next_turn()
signal battle_ended
notes: Combatant class needs: speed, is_dead, on_turn_start(). Use signals for UI updates on turn change.
ICombatant UInterface with GetSpeed(), IsDead(), OnTurnStart(). Use UMG widgets for action selection UI.
ItemData is a ScriptableObject with statModifiers list. CharacterStats uses a modifier stack (base + flat + percent).
ItemData extends Resource with stat_modifiers array. CharacterStats tracks base + modifier stack.
Uses GAS (Gameplay Ability System) for stat modifications. ItemData is a UDataAsset with TArray<TSubclassOf<UGameplayEffect>>.
Save meta-progression to persistent storage. Run-specific state resets on death; meta-state persists.
Saved as .tres resource in user://. Run state in a separate autoload that resets on death.
GameInstanceSubsystem persists across level loads. Use USaveGame for serialization.
Sync center and radius via network. Use phases array for multi-stage shrink. Minimap overlay shows zone.
Multiplayer: server authoritative zone state, replicate via RPC. Use MeshInstance3D cylinder for visual.
Server-authoritative: only HasAuthority() deals damage. Replicate CurrentRadius and ZoneCenter to clients for visual.
Check ConsumeSequence each frame for special move detection. bufferWindow of 0.1-0.2s is typical.
Call record_input from _input(). Check consume_sequence in _physics_process for special moves.
ActorComponent. Enhanced Input actions feed RecordInput(). Define move sequences in a DataTable.
Full lag compensation requires server-side hitbox rewinding. Use NetworkTime for clientTime. Mirror or Netcode for GameObjects.
Server authoritative: only server processes hit detection. Clients send fire RPC with local aim data.
Server RPC (ServerFire) ensures authority. MulticastShowImpact for all clients. Add lag compensation via rewinding.
Cap offline time to prevent exploits. Efficiency multiplier < 1.0 incentivizes active play. Show reward popup on login.

code:
# Offline progress
class_name OfflineProgress
static func calculate(rate_per_sec: float, last_played_unix: float) -> float:
    var elapsed = Time.get_unix_time_from_system() - last_played_unix
    elapsed = min(elapsed, 86400.0) # cap 24h
    return rate_per_sec * elapsed * 0.5 # 50% offline efficiency
static func apply_on_login(state: GameState):
    var earned = calculate(state.current_rate, state.last_logout)
    state.currency += earned
    state.last_logout = Time.get_unix_time_from_system()
    return earned # show in UI
notes: Store last_logout as Unix timestamp. Save state on _notification(NOTIFICATION_WM_CLOSE_REQUEST).
GameInstanceSubsystem for persistence. Broadcast delegate for UI popup. Cap at 24h to prevent exploits.
Trigger SpawnWave() on timer or player ready button. Scale wave count/interval for difficulty progression.

code:
# Wave spawner
class_name WaveSpawner extends Node
@export var waves: Array[WaveData] = []
@export var spawn_points: Array[Marker3D] = []
var current_wave: int = 0
func spawn_wave():
    if current_wave >= waves.size(): return
    var w = waves[current_wave]
    for i in w.count:
        var sp = spawn_points[i % spawn_points.size()]
        var enemy = w.enemy_scene.instantiate()
        add_child(enemy)
        enemy.global_position = sp.global_position
        await get_tree().create_timer(w.interval).timeout
    current_wave += 1
    wave_completed.emit(current_wave)
signal wave_completed(wave_num: int)
notes: WaveData is a Resource with enemy_scene, count, interval. Use await for spawn timing.
FWaveData in a DataTable for designer editing. Timer-based spawning avoids coroutines.
Buildings call RegisterIncome on placement. Units call Spend on training. UI subscribes to OnResourcesChanged.
Autoload singleton. Buildings register income on built, unregister on destroyed.
WorldSubsystem for per-world resource state. Use multicast delegate for UI binding.
UTC event timing. Persist to server for MP sync.

code:
extends Node
class_name LiveOpsManager
var active_event: String = ""
var event_end: float = 0.0
func start_event(id: String, hours: float) -> void:
    active_event = id
    event_end = Time.get_unix_time_from_system() + hours * 3600.0
func is_active() -> bool:
    return active_event != "" and Time.get_unix_time_from_system() < event_end
notes: Unix timestamps for offline-safe events.

code:
extends Node
class_name PassTracker
signal tier_up(tier: int)
var current_xp: int = 0
var current_tier: int = 0
var thresholds: Array[int] = [100,200,350,500]
func add_xp(amt: int) -> void:
    current_xp += amt
    while current_tier < thresholds.size() and current_xp >= thresholds[current_tier]:
        current_xp -= thresholds[current_tier]
        current_tier += 1
        tier_up.emit(current_tier)
notes: Signal on tier up for UI.
DataTable thresholds.

code:
extends Node
class_name UpgradeManager
var costs: Array[int] = [100,250,500]
var level: int = 0
func get_mult() -> float: return 1.0 + level * 0.2
func try_upgrade(wallet: Dictionary) -> bool:
    if level >= costs.size() or wallet.gold < costs[level]: return false
    wallet.gold -= costs[level]; level += 1; return true
notes: Dictionary wallet.

code:
extends Node
class_name RewardScheduler
var table: Array[Dictionary] = [{"id":"common","w":70},{"id":"rare","w":25},{"id":"epic","w":5}]
func roll() -> String:
    var total := 0.0
    for e in table: total += e.w
    var r := randf() * total
    var cum := 0.0
    for e in table:
        cum += e.w
        if r <= cum: return e.id
    return table[-1].id
notes: Weighted loot table.
DataTable rewards.
Faucet/sink inflation tracking.

code:
extends Node
class_name TokenEconomy
var bal: Dictionary = {}
var minted: int = 0
var sunk: int = 0
func mint(t: String, a: int):
    if not bal.has(t): bal[t] = 0
    bal[t] += a; minted += a
func sink(t: String, a: int) -> bool:
    if bal.get(t, 0) < a: return false
    bal[t] -= a; sunk += a; return true
notes: Multi-currency dictionary.
GameInstanceSubsystem. FName tokens.
Stock-limited with daily refresh.

code:
extends Node
class_name Storefront
var catalog: Array[Dictionary] = []
func buy(item: Dictionary, wallet: TokenEconomy) -> bool:
    if item.get("stock",0) > 0 and item.get("bought",0) >= item.stock: return false
    if not wallet.sink("gold", item.price): return false
    item.bought = item.get("bought",0) + 1; return true
func refresh():
    for item in catalog: item.bought = 0
notes: Dictionary catalog.

var mult: float = 1.0

func apply(v: float) -> float: return v * mult
notes: Soft reset. Multiplier compounds.

code:
extends Node
var _delay_ms: int = 120
var _jitter_ms: int = 40
var _loss_pct: float = 5.0
var _buffer: Array = []
func send_with_sim(peer_id: int, data: PackedByteArray) -> void:
    if randf() * 100.0 < _loss_pct: return
    var delay = (_delay_ms + randi_range(-_jitter_ms, _jitter_ms)) / 1000.0
    _buffer.append({"t": Time.get_ticks_msec()+delay*1000, "peer": peer_id, "data": data})
func _process(_d):
    while _buffer.size()>0 and _buffer[0].t<=Time.get_ticks_msec():
        multiplayer.send_bytes(_buffer.pop_front().data)
notes: Manual network condition simulation layer for Godot multiplayer.
Unity Performance Testing package with CI benchmark reporter for regression gating.
UE automation test measuring average tick time for CI performance gating.
GUT-based perf test measuring frame budget for CI gate checks.
